description: GWTTKB NFL Team Context — dynasty-relevant depth charts, scheme notes, and analyst commentary 2020-2026. Maintained by GWTTKB. Coach treats this as authoritative.

2020.notes: Hopkins was elite WR1. Murray mobile threat boosted SF value. Drake solid flex. Edmonds efficient receiving back.

2021.notes: Conner surprise breakout after leaving Pittsburgh. Hopkins still elite. Kirk finally found role & strong contributor. Murray MVP candidate. Edmonds great flex value.

2022.notes: Hopkins suspended 6 games. Hollywood Brown arrived then tore ACL. Murray torn ACL in December. McBride arrived as rookie — watch.

2023.notes: McBride emerged as legit TE1. Murray returned but limited. Hollywood Brown injury-plagued again. Rebuilding.

2024.notes: MHJ arrived — Kyler & MHJ struggled to find connection. McBride top 3 TE & established himself as the ELITE weapon but could not score TD with Murray at QB. Murray healthy. Offense clicked second half. But very dissapointing season

2025.notes: MHJ showed flashes of being a true WR1. McBride locked in as top TE. Pass-heavy scheme when Kyler Murray was hurt and soft benched the rest of the season. James Conner & Trey Benson suffered season ending injuries. Michael Wilson Broke out with Jacoby Brissett at QB.

2026.notes: Love arrives as 1.03 pick — immediate RB1 candidate. Mike LaFleur hired as new HC (Shanahan & McVay tree). OC is Nathaniel Hackett. Arizona signed Tyler Allgeier from Atlanta, will compete with James Conner for Backup. Expect Trey Benson COULD be traded or compete for backup. Cardinals drafted Carson Beck Round 3 of 2026 draft & will compete with Brissett who is looking for a new contract. Michael Wilson MAY establish himself as a TRUE WR2 in 2026 (Trending in right direction). MHJ WR1 breakout candidate, but has yet to demonstrate consistency.

2020.notes: Ridley emerged as legitimate WR1B. Julio elite but injury concerns. Gurley had knee issues. Gage underrated PPR option.

2021.notes: Julio Jones Traded in 2021 summer. Ridley injury and played 5 games. Pitts historic TE rookie. Patterson surprise flex hero. Gage PPR depth.

2022.notes: Ridley suspended entire season. London solid rookie. Pitts injuries limited him. Bad QB situation killed everyone's value. Allgeier sneaky late.

2023.notes: Bijan arrived — elite dynasty RB1 immediately. London steady. QB situation still awful. Taylor Heinicke replaced Ridder. Still bad. Allgeier useful handcuff.

2024.notes: Cousins torn achilles hurt dynasty production. Michael Penix Jr. mediocre 5 games after Cousins injury. Bijan great. London consistent, QB play limited breakout. Pitts still disappointing.

2025.notes: Penix takes over, offense increases, but Penix has some struggles. Penix tears ACL & Kirk Cousins becomes QB1. Young mobile QB could unlock London and scheme. Bijan elite. Pitts late season breakout earns franchise tag from Atlanta. Mooney very dissapointing.

2026.notes: Falcons signed Tua Tagovailoa after release from Dolphins. Penix may be ready week 1 but possible he is no longer the starter. This will be determined in camp. New HC: Kevin Stefanski from Browns. Tommy Rees is new OC. Followed Stefanski from Cleveland. Bijan & London are core offensive players. Pitts wants long term exension, but was franchise tagged (could be traded). Branch round 3 — Georgia WR adds speed & could produce early. Brian Robinson Jr is a valuable handcuff for Bijan Robinson but shouldn't have stand alone value. London clear WR1, Zaccheaus returns in free agency. Jahan Dotson (former 1st round pick) added in free agency. Zachariah Branch could see large volume as a rookie if develops quickly. QB play may still hold team back.

2020.notes: Andrews elite TE. Lamar SF value enormous. Hollywood Brown boom-bust. Dobbins promising breakout. Run heavy scheme. WR talent LACKING.

2021.notes: Andrews 1,361 yards — monster season. Hollywood inconsistent. Dobbins torn ACL preseason. No talented RB rotated Freeman, Murray, & even Leveon Bell. Lamar continued dominance & Andrews duo was unreal. Hollywood Brown had every opportunity to truly breakout, had GOOD season, but not elite.

2022.notes: Hollywood traded to ARI. Bateman underwhelming. Andrews elite again. Dobbins promising but injury-prone. Likely emerging TE2. Lamar injured & Tyler Huntley played 6 games.

2023.notes: Flowers electric rookie. Andrews injury. Lamar MVP. Mitchell electric before ACL. Likely solid TE2 value. Flowers excellent rookie year. OBJ dissapointed. Gus Edwards solid RB season.

2024.notes: Henry 1,900 yards absurd. Signed Henry in free agency & significantly boosted offense. Lamar Ridiculous 4172 passing yards, 41TD, 4 INT. Flowers steady. Lamar MVP again. Likely sneaky TE2 value. Flowers breakout but not true WR1. Bateman produced as solid deep threat. WR play still not elite. Mark Andrews struggled to produce with Likely playing well.

2025.notes: Lamar injured most of season. Played through injury. Tyler Huntley struggled as back up. Derrick Henry declined still posted 1595 rushing yards despite brutal team performance. Flowers ascending. Andrews declining. Lamar still elite SF value. Likely struggled to produce with offense struggles.

2026.notes: Lamar expected to completely bounce back. Derrick Henry is the unicorn who never ages apparently (fall off coming but production still here as of now). Ravens draft Adam Randall RB in 5th round. Justice Hill will still operate as 3rd down back. Henry main back. Isaiah Likely left to Giants in free agency. Andrews signs extension. Durham Smythe TE & Matthew Hibner TE also drafted. Andrews should be TE1 but unsure what production will look like. Ravens picked up Flowers franchise tag and he is under contract through 2027. Lane could be a rookie breakout, opportunity is 100% there.

2020.notes: Allen breakout SF monster. Diggs WR1 immediately. Knox emerging. Singletary and Moss split — neither dynasty relevant.

2021.notes: Knox 9 TDs. Diggs elite. Allen top 3 SF. Bills pass heavy — RBs worthless. Beasley PPR depth.

2022.notes: Diggs WR1 level. Davis boom-bust. Knox injuries. Allen still monster. Cook arriving as rookie late. James Cook had slow rookie season but overtook Singletary at the end of the season.

2023.notes: Cook breakout — 1,100 rush + receiving. Diggs remains clear WR1. Allen elite. Shakir emerges as productive slot WR. Gabe Davis productive WR3. Kincaiddominant rookie season. High hopes for him after second half of the season.

2024.notes: Diggs traded. Bills trade for Amari Cooper & he is solid. Shakir consistent. Cook maintained value. Allen still top SF asset. Coleman STRUGGLES with off field & lockerroom issues. Decently productive.

2025.notes: Keon Coleman looks like a complete bust. Multiple Healthy scratches because he showed up late to team meetings. Shakir reliable slot. Cook great value & very underrated. Allen locked as SF1 for years. Shakir was only reliable reciever. Kincaid struggled to stay Healthy & Knox is not consistent. Serious lack of production from pass catchers. Mainly Josh Allen & James Cook show in Buffalo this season. James Cook landed contract extension this offseason.

2026.notes: The Keon Coleman breakout may not ever happen. Bills traded for DJ Moore from Bears & under contract for 4 years. Large opportunity to emerge as main pass catcher in offense. Shakir reliable but no true ceiling yet. Allen 30 but still elite. Bills draft Skyler Bell WR from UConn 6 foor 192 LB 4.40 second 40 yard dash. Large opportunity for Bell in long run. Kincaid health remains a question. Opted to not get PCL surgery AGAIN & new HC Joe Brady says they will manage pain throughout 2026.

2020.notes: Christian McCaffrey injured 3 weeks into season. Moore emerged as legit WR1. Anderson solid. Samuel PPR depth. Terrible QB suppressed everything.

2021.notes: CMC injury again. Moore great despite bad QB. Anderson traded mid-season. Hubbard solid handcuff.

2022.notes: CMC traded to SF Week 7. Moore still produced. QB carousel. Foreman useful after CMC trade.

2023.notes: Moore traded in Bryce Young deal. Young struggled. Full rebuild. Mingo one of the worst WR of all time. Comical.

2024.notes: Young benched. Total rebuild. Hubbard fantasy relevant by default. Legette developmental. Diontae Johnson traded at deadline.

2025.notes: McMillan arrives 1.08 — immediate WR1 candidate. Bryce Young breakout, but not fantasy relevant yet. Panthers make playoffs. Tetairoa emerges as true WR1. Jalen Coker injured but when healthy is SOLID WR2. Xavier Legette is one of the worst 1st round WR of all time. Complete bust. Rico Dowdle overtook Chuba Hubbard as starter. Chuba had calf injury that he missed time for and played through. Chuba was incredibly inneficient. TE play is very rough. Sanders, Tremble, Evans all not providing any fantasy relevance.

2026.notes: McMillan year 2 — primary target share expected. Brazzell round 3 deep threat. Young has 5th year option picked up. Trending in the right direction. Hubbard serviceable, but the former 2nd round pick Jonathon Brooks is coming back from second ACL tear(great potential but all dynasty managers know). it will be interesting to see how Chris Brazzell II works into offense but expect Jalen Coker to be WR2. Xavier Legette may be traded early in the season. Panthers are very frustrated with lack of production. Carolina also may be in the market for a free agent TE like David Njoku (or may potentially trade for one). .

2020.notes: Robinson elite despite terrible QB. Montgomery 1,000 yards. Trubisky killed ceiling. Cohen torn ACL.

2021.notes: Fields took over mid-year. Robinson disappointed final season. Mooney quietly solid. Herbert flashed late.

2022.notes: Fields rushing dominance. Montgomery 1,200 rush yards. Mooney disappointing. Run heavy scheme limited WRs.

2023.notes: Moore arrived — finally good WR for Fields. Panthers traded HAUL to get 1st overall pick. Fields boom-bust. Herbert lightning when healthy. David Montgomery leaves for Lions. Mooney struggles to live up to potential with fields at QB.

2024.notes: Williams rookie struggled with protection. Moore steady. Offense struggled greatly as a whole. DJ Moore was solid, Keenan Allen left Chargers & was productive verteran. Kmet failed to produce well. Odunze struggled early in the season to find a role in the offense. Looked solid end of the season.

2025.notes: Williams year 2 expected leap. Ben Johnson elite new HC. Odunze primary target. DJ Moore ate up targets but was not main Weapon. Luther Burden MASSIVE breakout back half of season. Coleston Loveland led the team in recieving & completely broke out in the second half of the season. Swift very productive but aging. Kmet basically irrelevant. Kyle Monangai emerged as highly relevant 1B RB to D'Andre Swift. Swift still posted fantastic numbers, but Monangai showed serious flashes as being consistent back.

2026.notes: Strong disagreement who the WR1 will be in 2026. Odunze looked fantastic early in 2025, but Burden stole the show in the back half of the season. Loveland has emerged as Williams favorite target. Caleb Williams is set to have an excellent season again. RB room looks to remain the same with Swift 1A & Monangai 1B. Bears drafted TE Sam Roush in the 3rd round. Cole Kmet may be traded. Highly relevant fantasty offense.

2020.notes: Burrow torn ACL Week 10. Higgins strong despite QB change. Mixon solid. Boyd reliable PPR depth.

2021.notes: Chase historic rookie. Higgins WR2 level. Burrow elite. Mixon 1,200 yards. Full offense. Boyd PPR depth.

2022.notes: Chase injury midseason. Higgins stepped up. Burrow elite again. Mixon still 1,000 yards. Boyd PPR slot depth.

2023.notes: Burrow torn UCL Week 11. Jake Browning is the worst backup QB in NFL History and cannot support an offense. Chase/Higgins both excellent when healthy. Brown promising late. Boyd reliable.

2024.notes: Chase 1,700 yards elite. Higgins contract holdout then extended. Brown elite recieving back with high production. Burrow could have won MVP. Tee Higgins struggled to stay Healthy. Bengals defense struggled & Bengals were down in most games

2025.notes: Chase locked in as top dynasty WR. Brown proving himself. TE position weak. Burrow turf Toe Injury missed almost entire season. Joe Flacco traded to Bengals by Browns. Chase, Tee, Brown fantasy value saved. Could not produce with AWFUL QB play from Jake Browning (literally worst QB in NFL History). Bengals offensive line struggled. Defense struggled even more. One of the worst defenses in the NFL & Bengals couldnt make the playoffs.

2026.notes: Burrow and the Bengals look to get back on track & healthy for the 2026 season. Chase & Higgins are set to have fantastic years if the team is healthy. Chase Brown should see his fantasy value stay high in 2026 with Burrow back but it remains to be seen if he is the long term RB solution for Cincinatti. TE play does not typically produce consistent fantasy relevance. Bengals have added heavily on defense this offseason & should be more competitive. Worth note, if the Bengals do not play well this season HC Zac Taylor is going to be fired.

2020.notes: Chubb elite RB1. Hunt great handcuff. Landry steady. Baker mediocre. Run heavy scheme — Chubb dynasty cornerstone.

2021.notes: OBJ released mid-season. Chubb 1,200 yards. Baker declining. Njoku emerging. Hunt reliable handcuff.

2022.notes: Watson suspended 11 games after massive trade and record breaking contract. Cooper strong when Watson played. Chubb 1,500 yards. Njoku solid. Hunt handcuff value.

2023.notes: Chubb torn ACL Week 2. Watson mediocre. Cooper traded mid-season. Njoku consistent. Ford adequate backup.

2024.notes: Watson tore achilles. Complete rebuild mode. Jeudy traded to CLE from Denver but offense struggled. Njoku great but only with Winston.

2025.notes: Joe Flacco started the season as the starter afer Browns drafted Shedeur Sanders in the 5th round & Dillon Gabriel in the 3rd. Flacco eventually traded to Bengals. Gabriel started majority of the season and was AWFUL. Sheduer was decent when he played but also not fantasy relevant. Watson missed entire season recovering from torn Achilles. Only fantasy relevant player was Quinshon Judkins who missed the entire pre season due to legal issues. He eventually suffered brutal season ending injury. Dylan Sampson flashed but likely holds no value. Harold Fannin Jr. emerged as a fantastic NFL rookie TE with REAL upside.

2026.notes: Browns drafted OT Spencer Fano RD 1 & WR KC Concepcion RD 1 in 2026 draft. Round 2 they took Denzel Boston. Concepcion & Boston can have fantasy value IF Cleveland figures out QB position. Browns fired HC Stefanski & hired Jesse Minter. Jerry Jeudy will likely struggle to have real fantasy value with the new rookies introduced. Harold Fannin has strong value with David Njoku leaving Cleveland. Njoku is still a free agent & COULD potentially return to Cleveland but it is unlikely.

2020.notes: Dak torn ACL Week 5. CeeDee Lamb arrived as rookie — electric. Zeke declining. Cooper steady. Schultz emerging.

2021.notes: Lamb ascending. Cooper consistent. Zeke declining. Schultz breakout TE. Dak healthy. Pollard sneaky flex.

2022.notes: Lamb WR1 fully. Cooper traded. Pollard emerged — 1,000 yards. Zeke released after season. Ferguson watch.

2023.notes: Lamb elite WR1 — 1,749 yards. Ferguson breakout TE. Pollard decent. Cooks disappointing.

2024.notes: Dak torn Hamstring. Only played 8 games. Cooper rush struggled at QB. Rico Dowdle emerged as RB1 and Elliot returned to back him up but was washed. Ceedee Lamb played through injury most of the season but offense struggled. Jake Ferguson emerged as solid TE but QB play held the offense back.

2025.notes: Cowboys traded a 3rd round pick to the Steelers for George Pickens & he completely broke out. Ceedee Lamb missed time with injury & struggled with drops. Jevonte Williams was signed in free agency & was a top RB in the NFL (finally recovered from torn ACL, MCL, LCL, & PCL injury). Ryan Flournoy emerged as clear WR3 in Dallas offense.

2026.notes: Dallas has added significantly on defense this offseason and should be VERY competitive. Multiple games last year caused starters to be benched due to blow outs. Dak remains a highly productive agin QB with a well balanced pass and rush offense. Javonte Williams landed massive 3 year extension with Cowboys & George Pickens has been franchised tagged with no current plans to land long term extension. Ceedee Lamb looks to get back on track as a top 5 WR following a rough 2025. Pickens and Ceedee should both be viewed as Dynasty WR1s. Jake Ferguson is expected to also have a great 2026 season.

2020.notes: Jeudy disappointing rookie. Sutton injury early. Lock mediocre. Fant solid but gone after season. Patrick underrated.

2021.notes: Sutton back healthy. Jeudy injuries again. Gordon/Williams committee. Williams promising rookie. Bad QB situation.

2022.notes: Wilson disaster season. Williams torn ACL early. Sutton underused. Jeudy solid despite QB. Dulcich promising.

2023.notes: Wilson benched. Sutton and Jeudy wasted. Williams returned but limited. Sean Payton arrived. Mims flashed.

2024.notes: Nix solid rookie. Sutton finally had QB. Franklin promising. McLaughlin efficient. Payton scheme working.

2025.notes: Broncos offense BROKE OUT in 2025. Bo Nix emerged as GREAT low end QB1 in fantasy. WR room was difficult to predict other than Sutton (Bo Nix favorite target). Troy Franklin broke out as the WR2 and was a great deep threat. Broncos drafted RJ Harvey high in the draft but then signed JK Dobbins in free agency. Dobbins functioned as the RB1 & Harvey worked in. Looked good when in, but when Dobbins went down & went to IR, Harvey was highly productive in the back half of the season. Evan Engram was a WALKING TRASH CAN and one of the biggest dissapointments in the entire NFL. Pat Bryant looked solid & so did Marvin Mims Jr as depth players.

2026.notes: Bo Nix will be 100% healthy by camp following foot injury in NFL playoffs. Broncos traded their 2026 1st round pick for Dolphins WR Jaylin Waddle. This will significantly reduce Sutton's role as the alpha in the offense. Pat Bryant & Troy Franklin will likely have trade interest but if they stay on the team will have reduced roles. Broncos backfield is incredibly difficult to project & will need to be monitored all summer. Denver extended JK Dobbins & Drafted Jonah Coleman in the 5th round. Dobbins expected to be 1A and Harvey 1B, with Coleman as reserve, but Sean Payton as HC can do whatever he wants. Broncos drafted TE Justin Joly in the 5th round who has a small chance to be relevant because Evan Engram is legit trash

2020.notes: Golladay 1,000 yards then injury. Swift promising rookie. Hockenson solid TE. Marvin Jones consistent.

2021.notes: Stafford traded. ARSB emerged from nowhere. Swift great when healthy. Hockenson traded mid-season.

2022.notes: ARSB 1,161 yards elite slot. Swift 1,000 yards. Goff competent. Lions ascending. Williams 17 TDs as goal line back.

2023.notes: LaPorta elite rookie TE. Montgomery + Gibbs elite RB duo. ARSB consistent. Lions legit contender.

2024.notes: Gibbs 1,000+ every way. Williams breakout. ARSB elite. LaPorta solid. Best offense in NFL.

2025.notes: Gibbs elite dynasty RB. Williams ascending WR2. ARSB still WR1. LaPorta consistent but suffered serious back injury that caused him to miss the half of the 2025 season. Montgomery took a back seat to Gibbs but was productive when he played. Offense struggled more than usual & missed the playoffs unexpectedly. Goff remains a low end QB1 with high upside because of the high powered oppportunity for Lions to drop 50 points in any given game. Raymond functioned as a gadget WR3 and held little value while TeSlaa showed serious flashes and was a red zone power house.

2026.notes: Gibbs + Williams + ARSB all dynasty assets. David Montgomery was traded to the Houston Texans & opens the opportunity for Gibbs to be completely featured in this offense. If he can stay healthy, he may be the RB1 in fantasy for years to come. Lions brought in Isiah Pacheco as RB2 from the Chiefs on a 1 year deal. He is not expected to play very much, but could be relevant with Gibbs injury. Khalif Raymond heads to Chicago Bears & Kendrick Law COULD emerge as a solid WR4 or WR3 in the long run. Isaac TeSlaa holds significant value if St Brown or Williams were injured. Williams & St Brown are both excellent assets. Goff remains a interesting aging QB that is a low end QB1 in fantasy with a VERY high weekly ceiling but no mobility upside.

2020.notes: Adams elite WR1. Jones elite RB1. Tonyan 11 TDs. Rodgers MVP. Dillon bruiser backup. Stack this offense.

2021.notes: Adams WR1 all year. Rodgers MVP again. Jones 1,000 yards. MVS boom-bust. Tonyan torn ACL. Dillon goal line.

2022.notes: Adams traded to LV. Rodgers struggled to produce with Lazard & rookie Christian Watson. Romeo Doubs started to break out at the end of the season. Aaron Jones was a highly producive PPR back.

2023.notes: Love took over — threw for over 4k yards in first season as starter. Watson injuries & Aaron Jones injuries. Jayden Reed emerged as WR1, with Doubs having some form of a breakout. Wicks looked electric but struggled with drops. AJ Dillon was terribly inefficient.

2024.notes: Love solid. Reed consistent slot. GB signs Josh Jacobs in free agency & let Aaron Jones walk. Packers draft promising backup RB MarShawn Lloyd who could not stay healthy. Emmanuel Wilson emerges as solid backup RB. Tucker Kraft emerges as premium TE in the NFL. Fantastic player. Jordan Love plays well but remains low end QB1 in fantasy with high volatility.

2025.notes: Reed missed extended time with injury and only played 7 games. Doubs emerged as WR1 with Christian Watson showing flashes of being a true X/Deep threat WR at times. Tucker Kraft tore ACL & missed half of the season. Josh Jacobs regressed but so did the entire offense. Malik Willis played 4 games with Jordan Love missing time due to injury and BALLED OUT. Packers drafted Matthew Golden in the 1st round of the NFL draft & he did not get involved much on the offense. Golden looked good when he got the ball, but was not a massive part of the game plan

2026.notes: Key departures: Romeo Doubs to New England Patriots, Wicks to Eagles. Massive opportunity for Watson, Golden, and Reed. We will have to see who plays the most on 2 WR sets. Reed landed 3 year 50m extension with packers. Golden could be primed for a breakout. Kraft should be recovered from torn ACL early in the season & Musgrave will fill in if he misses time but no true upside to him. Savion Williams will compete for WR4 but it is unlikely he holds any true value. Jordan Love needs to stay healthy but could be primed for a great 2026 season. Josh Jacobs is showing signs for concern with efficiency & managers need to think about him, however, packers have not brought in any competition so in the meantime he should be ok.

2020.notes: Hopkins traded to ARI. Watson elite despite that. Cooks surprisingly solid. Will Fuller boom-bust before suspension.

2021.notes: Watson suspended. Mills mediocre. Cooks still produced. Collins flashed as rookie. Jordan promising TE.

2022.notes: Pierce 939 yards as rookie. Collins developing. Cooks steady. Full rebuild. Jordan 37 catches.

2023.notes: Stroud elite rookie. Collins WR1 level. Nathaniel Dell emerging late. Pierce fading. Dell dynasty watch.

2024.notes: Collins injured but WR1 when healthy. Diggs arrived & tore ACL. Dell brutal injury. Mixon efficient. Dell promising. Stroud solid. Contender window.

2025.notes: Collins back healthy — WR1. Dell misses entire season due to 2024 knee injury. Joe Mixon misses entire season with foot injury (shot himself in the foot is the word on the street). Pass game struggled because Stroud regressed behind HORRENDOUS O line play. Drafted Higgins & Noel in 2nd & 3rd round. Higgins showed a lot of promise. Could progress. Nick Chubb regressed heavily (cooked). Woody Marks showed a lot of promise as a rookie RB. Strong recieving back. Not projected to be workhorse back.

2026.notes: Nico Collins remains a high producing dynasty WR when CJ Stroud plays well. Texans have to improve O line play for offense to provide large fantasy relevance. Traded for David Montgomery from the Lions & He will operate as RB1 with Woody Marks expected to also work in. CJ Stroud has struggled since his rookie season & is a mid range QB2 most weeks in fantasy. Texans do expect to extend him long term despite poor play. Jayden Higgins is an interesting year 2 breakout candidate but Tank Dell also returns in 2026 (We will see if his knee still works).

2020.notes: JT arrived — incredible rookie. Hilton fading. Rivers managed game. JT dynasty cornerstone. Hines PPR depth.

2021.notes: JT 1,811 yards dominant. Pittman emerged as solid WR. Wentz decent. Hines receiving back value.

2022.notes: JT 1,000 yards despite QB chaos. Pittman consistent. Ryan benched. Pierce developmental.

2023.notes: Richardson electric before injury. JT 1,000 yards. Pittman solid. Downs promising late.

2024.notes: Richardson inconsistent but dynamic. Joe Flacco elevated offense when played. JT struggled with injuries at end of season but very productive. Pittman steady. Downs solid slot. Alex Pierce led the team in recieving. Emerged as premier deep threat WR. Mitchell developing slowly.

2025.notes: Colts sign Daniel Jones who beats out Anthony Richardson in camp. Alec Pierce emerges as ELITE deep threat WR. Pittman Jr. productive but takes back seat behind Pierce. Tyler Warren drafted in the 1st round & makes immediate impact. Jonathan Taylor was on pace for historic season before Daniel Jones tears achilles. Replaced Jones with Philip Rivers (offense struggled). Josh Downs struggled with injuries but highly productive slot WR. DJ Giddens pure back up to Taylor. Taylor workhorse back. Mitchell traded to the Jets.

2026.notes: Anthony Richardson will be traded or released. Daniel Jones on track for full recovery from torn achilles & landed extension. Very positive for offense. Jonathan Taylor aging but HIGHLY productive. Alec Pierce lands MASSVIVE 4 year contract & Colts invested in him as their WR1. Josh Downs is primed for an increase workload & could truly break out in 2026. Michael Pittman Jr. traded to Steelers. Tyler Warren is a top TE in dynasty & could have a MASSIVE season next year.

2020.notes: Robinson undrafted gem — 1,070 yards. Chark solid. Minshew fun but limited. Shenault developmental.

2021.notes: Lawrence struggled under Urban Meyer disaster. Robinson torn achilles. Chark solid early. Marvin Jones consistent.

2022.notes: Lawrence breakout. Kirk WR1 level. Engram 73 catches. Etienne 1,000+ yards. Jones and Shenault depth.

2023.notes: Engram 114 catches — elite. Ridley disappointing but still hit 1,000 yards. Etienne poductive due to volume. Etienne 1,000 yards. Lawrence solid. Bigsby promising. Krik productive before injury. Parker Washington quiet season only played 9 games.

2024.notes: BTJ electric rookie WR1. Lawrence shoulder injury mid season. Mac Jones fills in & Brian Thomas Jr posts INSANE rookie season. True rookie break out. Etienne STRUGGLED. Parker Washington emerges as WR2 behind Thomas. Christian Kirk awful. Engram fading. Brenton Strange breaks out at TE behind Engram

2025.notes: Jaguars trade future first to acquire Travis Hunter who has slow start to season. Posts 1 massive game & injured for rest of season. Jaguars trade for Jakobi Meyers from Raiders & extend 3 years. Operates as 1b WR. Brian Thomas Jr. has worst sophmore slump season to record & struggles with drop & production issues. Parker Washington leads team in recieving & has breakout from WR3 position. Travis Etienne Jr. has career turn around & has his best season of career. Brenton Strange struggled with injury but productive when healthy. Trevor Lawrence shows promise as QB but still not QB1 on weekly basis for fantasy, could become a QB1 though.

2026.notes: Travis Etienne Jr. has left the Jaguars in free agency for the Saints. Opening door for Bhayshul Tuten to operate as team lead back. Jaguars sign Chris Rodriguez Jr. to 2 year deal from Commanders. Jaguars draft Nate Boerkircher (TE) in 2nd round and should cause competition for Brenton Strange. Also drafted Josh Cameron & CJ Williams both WR in the 6th round. Travis Hunter is expected to play more CB than WR in 2026, but we will need confirmation through camp (very  bad for dynasty value if true). Brian Thomas Jr looks to return to his rookie form as a true WR1 but Jakobi Meyers may operate as the teams 1A. Parker Washington has potential to be massive contributor in 2026 if BTJ struggles continue.

2020.notes: Kelce still elite. Hill WR1. CEH disappointing. Mahomes elite SF. Stack KC offense. Reid uses TEs.

2021.notes: Kelce 1,125 yards. Hill elite. CEH still disappointing. Mahomes top SF asset. Williams filled in well.

2022.notes: Hill traded. Kelce 1,338 yards monster. JuJu solid. Pacheco emerged late. Mahomes SB MVP.

2023.notes: Rice breakout WR. Kelce declining slightly. Pacheco 1,000 yards. Hardman back. Noah Gray sneaky TE2.

2024.notes: Rice Knee injury. Worthy explosive rookie but did not meet expectations. Kelce fading fast but still productive. Pacheco solid but inneficient & got injured. Kareem Hunt operated as RB1. Mahomes still wins. Hollywood brown played 2 games due to injury. WR play struggled without Rice.

2025.notes: Worthy struggled again to meet expectations. Rice returned following suspension & was highly productive. Hollywood Brown retruned from injury & was not consistent. Does not have long term value. Kelce looked awful but still produced due to volume but is washed at this point. Pacheco 1A & Hunt 1B. Both AWFUL RB & difficult to start in fantasy. Mahomes dynasty value declining with age & ACL tear end of season but still elite SF. Jalen Royals drafted & produced almost nothing but interesting prospect.

2026.notes: Rashee Rice is slotted to operate as WR1 & holds high value for 2026 season. Mahomes should be recovered from torn ACL. Kelce is in his final season most likely. Xavier Worthy has struggled to produce, could break out, but not looking great right now. Chiefs sign Kenneth Walker III in free agency (reigning superbowl MVP RB from seahawks) and he is set for monster season. Brashard Smith should operate as receiving back and Emmett Johnson will battle for 3rd string back with Emari Demercado.

2020.notes: Herbert electric rookie. Ekeler great receiving RB. Allen reliable. Mike Williams boom-bust. Guyton deep threat.

2021.notes: Herbert elite sophomore. Allen healthy 1,000 yards. Williams 1,146 yards. Ekeler top flex. Stack this.

2022.notes: Williams torn ACL Week 1. Allen 1,000 yards. Ekeler still great. Herbert elite. Palmer sleeper.

2023.notes: Keenan and Williams both aging. Ekeler aging and production FALLS OFF A CLIFF. Herbert mediocre without weapons. Quentin Johnson VERY dissapointing rookie season.

2024.notes: McConkey breakout rookie. Dobbins efficient when healthy. Harbaugh run-first limits passing. Herbert managed. Quentin Johnson better sophomore season than many realized. Keenan Allen & Mike Williams left opening up opportunity for other players.

2025.notes: Omarion Hampton drafted 1st round. Many expected strong run game, but injuries to Slater & Alt on O line cause Chargers to throw WAY MORE than expected. Quentin Johnson BREAKOUT first half of season but faded off later. Keenan Allen returned & saw significant volume causing Ladd McConkey production to drop significantly. Ladd was dissapointing. Herbert produced well. Vidal was a great 1B RB & filled in with Hampton down. Hampton had a very dissapointing rookie season but showed promise. Tre' Harris dissapointing rookie season and underused. KeAndre Lambert-Smith gained pre season hype but did not produce at all. TE Oronde Gadsden broke out & will be the Chargers TE of the future.

2026.notes: Chargers bring in Mike McDaniel as offensvie coordinator who could SUPERCHARGE offense. Herbert ascending as top 7 dynasty QB. Keenan Allen remains a free agent. Ladd & QJ should operate as 1A & 1B with Ladd having more potential. Tre' Harris should operate as WR3 if no one else is brought in. TE Oronde Gadsden could be very good. RB room is complex. Hampton is the presumed starter week 1 with massive potential but Vidal has been very capable as a back up & chargers also signed Keaton Mitchell to 2 year deal in free agency. Question mark people should think about in usage. Thompson round 4.

2020.notes: Akers torn achilles Week 15. Kupp consistent. Woods reliable. Goff managed game well. Higbee steady TE.

2021.notes: Kupp 1,947 yards all-time season. Stafford upgrade massive. Woods torn ACL. Akers returned playoff.

2022.notes: Kupp injury. Stafford injury. Akers never recaptured form. Disaster year. ARob massive disappointment.

2023.notes: Nacua 1,486 yards historic rookie. Williams 1,000 yards. Kupp injured again. Puka dynasty buy.

2024.notes: Nacua WR1 fully. Williams 1,200 yards. Kupp decline phase. Stafford old but producing at high level. McVay system elite.

2025.notes: Nacua is a dynasty cornerstone. Rams sign Davante Adams & he produces at high level when healthy (especially in red zone). Stafford aging but wins MVP. Blake Corum worked in & eroded Kyren Williams dynasty value. Much more of a split than many people thought it would be. Cooper Kupp left for Seahawks.

2026.notes: Rams draft Ty Simpson in the 1st round but Stafford is rumored to be signing 2 year extension. Simpson likely to sit until 2028 season. Best for him for development. Stafford will play at MVP level until retires more than likely. Puka has had off field trouble and spent time in Rehab, but should be ready by week 1. Rams draft TE Max Klare early & will likely be TE1 ahead of Parkinson but may not be. RB room is confusing, Corum has potential to overtake Kyren Williams in 2026 season. Both backs hold high value if the other is hurt.

2020.notes: Waller elite TE. Jacobs 1,000 yards. Ruggs promising. Agholor surprisingly efficient. Henry Ruggs boom-bust.

2021.notes: Ruggs killed in DUI accident Week 8. Waller injuries. Jacobs limited use. Carr adequate.

2022.notes: Adams arrived — WR1 production. Jacobs 1,516 yards. Waller traded after season. Renfrow injury.

2023.notes: Adams wasted on bad offense. Jacobs 1,000 yards. QB chaos killed everything. Mayer disappointing rookie.

2024.notes: Bowers 1,194 yards — elite TE2 rookie. Adams traded mid-season. QB still terrible. Total rebuild.

2025.notes: Bowers struggled with injuries but still TE1 in dynasty. Tre Tucker showed flashes of being a contributor on offense. Geno Smith was a WALKING TRASH CAN in this offense. One of the worst offensive lines of all time. Ashton Jeanty struggled behind terrible offensive line. Looked good on some opportunities and still have high hope for top 5 RB. Jach Bech was a massive rookie disapointment and Dont'e Thornton Jr. MAY not be able to play football (Trash).

2026.notes: Mendoza 1.01 — franchise QB of the future. Raiders also signed Kirk Cousins & he is assumed week 1 starter. Mendoza may not play early half of season unless beats Cousins out in camp. Raiders spent big in free agency and O line should be better but still bad. Buy Bowers long-term. Monitor Mendoza development. Raiders will probably sign a veteran WR who could operate as WR1, either Jauan Jennings or Diggs potentially. Tyreek Hill also an option. Mike Washington Jr will back up Ashton Jeanty. New HC Klint Kubiak former Seahawks OC should also boost offense creativity.

2020.notes: Tua arrived late. Gaskin efficient. Parker solid. Gesicki receiving TE with no run block. Grant dynamic returner.

2021.notes: Waddle electric rookie. Tua limited. Gesicki 73 catches. McDaniel coming to change everything. Fuller bust.

2022.notes: Hill 1,710 yards. Tua healthy with McDaniel = elite offense. Waddle 1,356 yards. Mostert efficient. Stack.

2023.notes: Achane historic efficiency. Hill 1,799 yards. Tua health concern. Waddle solid. Mostert 800 yards.

2024.notes: Tua concussions again. Hill still produced. Waddle solid. Achane 1,000 yards. Jonnu 67 catches.

2025.notes: Tua looked absolutely AWFUL at QB eventually being benched for Ewers. Tyreek Hill blew up knee looking at 12+ month recovery. Achane absolutely ELITE RB. Jaylen Waddle operated as teams WR1 once Tyreek Hill went down. Malik Washington is not good but had production due to volume. Darren Waller (old) was productive but hurt all the time. Greg Dulcich was productive TE.

2026.notes: Dolphins sign Malik Willis in free agency to 3 year deal. Mobile QB with high fantasy upside. Offense skill positions (other than Achance) now worst in NFL. Unsure who starting WR will be. Dolphins drafted Caleb Douglas & Chris Bell (both have very good potential). Malik Washington is not good, Tutu Atwell unlikely to be highly productive, Terrace Marshall & Turpin also signed. Both are also not good. Greg Dulcich does have potential if Willis can get the offense working

2020.notes: Jefferson historic rookie. Cook elite dual-threat. Thielen still produced. Smith Jr promising TE backup.

2021.notes: Jefferson 1,616 yards. Cook 1,100 rush + receiving. Cousins efficient. Thielen consistent. KJ Osborn PPR depth.

2022.notes: Jefferson 1,809 yards near-record. Hockenson arrived mid-season via trade. Cook released. Stack.

2023.notes: Jefferson torn ACL Week 1. Addison promising. Hockenson torn ACL. Mattison disappointing. Addison showed out.

2024.notes: Darnold surprise Pro Bowl season. Jefferson elite. Addison solid. Jones 1,000 yards. Hockenson returned.

2025.notes: JJ Nine McCarthy turned out to be one of the worst QBs to ever play football. The offense literally could't do anything with him at QB. Wentz was not much better. Max Brosmer was straight BUTT. Jefferson & Addison struggled to produce but that was it was out of their control. TJ Hockenson struggled and did not look good. Nailor was a productive WR3 & showed a lot of promise. Disaster season. Jones could not stay healthy & regressed while Jordan Mason was inneficient with increased opportunity.

2026.notes: Jefferson dynasty cornerstone regardless of QB. Vikings sign Kyler Murray from Cardinals who is looking to turn around career. Kyler will compete with McCarthy for QB1 spot but JJ Nine McCarthy is BOOTY CHEEKS, so Kyler will start & could elevate offense a lot. Jefferson long term value there regardless of QB, Addison is great WR but volume still isnt there, interesting buy if can get him for cheap. Aaron Jones may phase out and took a pay cut to stay. Mason is mid. Rookie RB Claiborne COULD build out a role if he can beat out Mason. Hockenson should be better than last year but his play has declined a lot.

2020.notes: Covid season. Belichick offense terrible. No dynasty relevant players. White PPR depth.

2021.notes: Mac Jones solid rookie. Harris 1,000 yards. Henry 9 TDs. Meyers reliable. Jonnu Smith disappointment.

2022.notes: Stevenson 1,040 yards. Meyers consistent. Mac Jones regression. Henry reliable. Full rebuild after Belichick.

2023.notes: Complete disaster. Mac benched. No dynasty assets worth owning. Stevenson by default.

2024.notes: Maye arrived late. Stevenson still got work. Maye started after 8 games and looked very solid. He was the 3rd overall pick with lots of potential. WR play lacked. Boutte & Douglas showed flashes to be contributors. Bourne was a solid WR as well. Polk was a complete bust & complete BOOTY. Gibson operated as RB2.

2025.notes: Maye full season — top dynasty QB target. Stefon Diggs emerged as WR1 & had highly productive season. Hunter Henry Drake Maye favorite TE and was inconsistent, but productive. Boutte showed flashes of a breakout but it never came. Mack Hollins was productive but didnt hold value. Henderson did not play much & struggled first half of season but eventually overtook Stevenson as RB1. Stevenson still played a major part in the offense despite worse play. Drake Maye emerged as #1 dynasty asset potential with ELITE upside & consistency.

2026.notes: Maye dynasty franchise QB. Patriots signed Romeo Doubs from Packers (huge potential and solid player could be WR2). The Patriots WILL trade for AJ Brown from Eagles & he will be their WR1 next year. He has MASSIVE potential with Drake Maye. Henderson should operate as lead back in 2026, but Vrabel has done strange things with playing time for backs & Stevenson will work in. Henry is still TE1, but drafted Raridon round 3 TE — Vrabel uses TEs.

2020.notes: Kamara 21 TDs Christmas game record. Thomas injury. Brees final season. Kamara dynasty elite. Cook reliable.

2021.notes: Thomas injury again. Winston 14 TDs before torn ACL. Kamara still reliable. Receiving WRs wasted.

2022.notes: Olave electric rookie. Thomas finally healthy but limited. Kamara solid. Bad QB situation. Shaheed deep threat.

2023.notes: Olave 1,042 yards. Thomas released. Kamara aging. Carr decent upgrade. Olave dynasty WR2. Shaheed speed.

2024.notes: Olave injury. Shaheed dynamic deep threat. Kamara final relevant year. Carr mediocre. Rebuild needed.

2025.notes: Carr retired. Rattler struggled early & replaced by Shough who BALLED OUT. Shaheed traded to Seahawks mid season. Full rebuild mode. Kamara washed, hurt & not productive. Kendre Miller showed promise but got hurt Devin Neal & Audric Estime filled in. Chris Olave was hihgly productive. Amazing year and fully healthy (super slept on). Vele showed promise for potential breakout.

2026.notes: Tyson 1.08 pick — immediate target share in rebuilding offense. Significantly hurts Olave potential, but Olave is highly talented WR. Shough surrounded with Weapons in New Orleans by signing Travis Etienne Jr. in free agency (lots of potential to be high producer). Alvin Kamara value is toast. Interested to see how Olave & Tyson split target share & if the rest of the offense other than Etienne holds fantasy value.

2020.notes: Saquon torn ACL Week 2. Without him Giants worthless. Jones mediocre. Engram inconsistent.

2021.notes: Saquon returned but limited. Toney flashed then injuries. Golladay massive disappointment.

2022.notes: Saquon 1,312 yards. Daboll arrival transformed Jones. Robinson promising before ACL. Better vibes.

2023.notes: Jones torn ACL. Waller torn ACL. Saquon carried. Disaster injuries. Slayton steady despite chaos.

2024.notes: Nabers elite rookie despite terrible QB. Jones benched/cut. Nabers dynasty value rises to top 3 wr. Wan'Dale Robinson highly productive.

2025.notes: Wilson starts the season & benched for 1st round rookie Jaxon Dart. Dart absolutely electric start to rookie season posting GREAT fantasy numbers. Cam Skattebo highly productive RB, but injured. Malik Nabers tore ACL week 2. Wan'Dale Robinson emerged as WR1 & completely broke out. Tracey value is out the window, won't be highly productive player. Filled in fine, but not the future in NY. Slayton does not have dynasty value.

2026.notes: Giants fired Daboll & brought in Harbaugh. Harbaugh should revamp offense. Brought it Isaiah Likely from Baltimore who seriously hurts Theo Johnson dynasty value (likely should be good). NY did not draft RB & Skattebo will be the starter week 1 of 2026 unless they add someone. He could have a monster season with his Jaxson Dart connection growing. Jaxson Dart gets to play with Malik Nabers for the first time & connection should be electric. Darnell Mooney is a great signing & is on a 1 year prove it deal in NY. If Dart play continues, Mooney will have value.

2020.notes: Complete rebuild. No dynasty assets. Tank season. Crowder PPR depth.

2021.notes: Wilson disappointing. Moore flashed before injury. Carter PPR sleeper. Bad team.

2022.notes: G Wilson electric rookie. Breece Hall elite before torn ACL Week 7. Garrett Wilson dynasty hold.

2023.notes: Rodgers torn ACL Week 1. Hall 1,100 yards. G Wilson consistent despite QB carousel. Sad season.

2024.notes: Rodgers full season. Hall disappointment. G Wilson steady. Williams injury again. Mediocre offense.

2025.notes: Fields disaster. Brutal season. Wilson hurt most of season. While ELITE WR, cannot produce with HORRIBLE QB play. Hall failed to meet expectations again with Braelon Allen & Isaiah Davis eating into carries. Mitchell had flashes as a WR2, but lacked consistency. John Metchie III played decent. Mason Taylor played very well.

2026.notes: Fields traded. Jets bring in Geno Smith from Raiders who was AWFUL in Vegas last year. Could provide decent opportunity for Garrett Wilson (elite WR needs better QB play). Adonai Mitchell likely to play WR3 behind Omar Cooper Jr (round 1 pick). Will have to monitor closely to see. Drafted Kenyon Sadiq Round 1 TE (high ceiling but may take time to break out). Mason Taylor will likely be TE2 behind Sadiq but could be starter at beginning of season. Breece Hall playing on franchise tag. The talent is there, but the offense has to be able to score for Breece to be productive.

2020.notes: Wentz disaster season. Jalen Hurts took over. Sanders solid. Ertz traded. Goedert emerging TE.

2021.notes: Hurts mobile monster. Smith WR1 immediately. Goedert 830 yards. Sanders 1,000 yards. Rising team.

2022.notes: AJ Brown arrived elite. Smith WR1B. Goedert 702 yards. Hurts MVP candidate. Best offense.

2023.notes: AJ Brown 1,456 yards. Smith 1,066 yards. Swift disappointing. Goedert injury. Still top offense.

2024.notes: Saquon 2,005 yards all-time season. AJ Brown injury. Smith elite. Hurts SB MVP. Dynasty gold mine.

2025.notes: Saquon dynasty sell high if possible. AJ Brown Trade Rumors & Inconsistency. Goedert scored tons of TD & was productive if scored. Smith ascending. Hurts struggled but produced well in fantasy.

2026.notes: AJ Brown is going to be traded to the Patriots. He will not be on the team. Devonta Smith is primed to BREAKOUT as a WR1 IF he can maintain target share. Drafted Makai Lemon Round 1, Eli Stowers (great receiving TE who was also scouted as WR). Eagles also traded for Dontayvion Wicks from Packers who is a talented WR but hasn't put it all together yet. Signed Marquiese Brown in free agency who is expected to operate as WR3/4. There are a lot of mouths to feed on this offense and not all of them can be fantasy relevant. Saquon had a rough 2025 season but should be great in 2026 IF Jalen Hurts can improve his level of play. There are a lot of targets available with AJ Brown leaving.

2020.notes: JuJu solid. Diontae 88 catches. Claypool boom-bust. Big Ben declining. Najee Harris coming.

2021.notes: Najee 1,200 yards as rookie. Diontae 107 catches. Freiermuth promising TE. Big Ben final year. JuJu decent.

2022.notes: Najee 1,000 yards. Diontae 86 catches. Claypool traded. Pickett took over late. Pickens emerging.

2023.notes: Pickens emerging. Johnson traded. Najee 1,035 yards. Pickett mediocre. Pickens dynasty buy.

2024.notes: Pickens 1,000+ yards despite QB chaos. Fields dynamic. Najee declining. Freiermuth solid.

2025.notes: Pickens traded to Cowboys & did not play for Steelers in 2025. Steelers traded for DK Metcalf & he had dissapointing season. Opportunity was there but DK struggled to be a weekly WR2. Still potential in Metcalf. Calvin Austin, Roman Wilson, and Thielen were irrelevant for fantasy. Pat Freiermuth had an up and down year, struggling to be fully utilized with Jonnu Smith & Darnell Washington also seeing lots of playing time. Jaylen Warren was a highly productive back & so was Kenneth Gainwell. Both backs had fantastic seasons & were viable in fantasy. Kenneth Gainwell even was playable as flex/RB2.

2026.notes: Bernard round 2 — Steelers traded up. Physical WR2. Steelers traded for Michael Pittman Jr. who is aging but will elevate the passing game. DK Metcalf looks to get back on track in 2026 & establish himself as WR1. Crowded WR room. Steelers drafted WR Riley Nowakowski in the 4th round & he will likely operate as WR5 or return specialist. Signed Rico Dowdle to 2 year deal in free agency. Warren is slotted to be 1A and Dowdle 1B, but any injury to either back makes the other a home run hit in fantasy for 2026. Aaron Rodgers SHOULD return to Steelers, but also drafted Drew Allar (bad QB but good prospect who should be rostered because he can progress)

2020.notes: Wilson elite. Metcalf 1,303 yards. Lockett 100 catches. Carson solid. Stack SEA. Olsen solid veteran.

2021.notes: Wilson injury. Carson injury. Metcalf 967 yards. Lockett consistent. Disappointing year. Penny flashed.

2022.notes: Geno surprise Pro Bowl. Metcalf 1,048 yards. Lockett 995 yards. Penny injury again. Fant solid.

2023.notes: Metcalf 1,114 yards. Lockett 894 yards. Charbonnet efficient rookie. Geno adequate. JSN drafted.

2024.notes: JSN disappointing sophomore. Metcalf trade rumors. Charbonett & Walker eat into each other fantasy value. Macdonald new HC.

2025.notes: JSN WR1 breakout, top dynasty asset. Sam Darnold fantastic season, low end QB1 option weekly. No mobility so lower floor. Charbonet tore ACL & Kenneth Walker III finished superbowl MVP. DK Metcalf traded & Lockett didnt play in Seattle in 2025. Traded for Shaheed productive deep threat with high ceiling but very low floor. Kupp WAY BEYOND prime. Retiring soon. Tory Horton COULD have upside if he sees playing time increase. Does not heavily utilize TE. Barner fine, Arroyo shows long term potential & very athletic.

2026.notes: Price round 1 RB — bell cow opportunity. with Charbonnet recovering from torn ACL. Lose OC Kubiak to Raiders. JSN top dynasty asset (ELITE). Shaheed could see expanded role in 2026 with full offseason with team. Charbonnet has never truly broken out & torn ACL places concern on productivity when back from injury. Cooper Kupp is dust & totally cooked. Tory Horton could be a break out candidate in 2026. Darnold needs to be rostered in all formats & decent low end QB1 with a high powered offense.

2020.notes: Kittle 1,000 yards. Aiyuk promising rookie. Mostert injury. Shanahan system elevates everyone. Bourne slot depth.

2021.notes: Deebo 1,405 yards as WR/RB hybrid. Aiyuk 826 yards. Mitchell efficient. Kittle health concern.

2022.notes: CMC arrived Week 7 — immediate RB1. Purdy electric. Aiyuk ascending. Deebo usage changed. Stack everything.

2023.notes: CMC 1,459 yards best RB. Aiyuk 1,342 yards. Purdy elite. Kittle 1,020 yards. SB appearance. Mason sneaky flex.

2024.notes: CMC injury-plagued. Aiyuk contract holdout. Purdy solid. Kittle aging. Deebo declining. Mason stepped up.

2025.notes: CMC elite performance & played all 17 games. Brock Purdy struggled with injury & Mac Jones filled in proving he can be a starter in the NFL. WR production limited & offense funneled through CMC & Kittle mainly. Jennings high end WR3, Ricky Pearsall struggled to stay healthy all season & did not have a great year. Opportunity to break out later. Kendrick Bourne was productive but inconsistent. Brian Robinson was a back up only. Bandon Aiyuk missed entire season due to ACL tear & relationship with south with 49ers front office. No future with the team. George Kittle torn achilles end of season.

2026.notes: Stribling round 2 has opportunity to break out early. Mike Evans signing in free agency. Should be alpha WR1 but aging & injury risk. Potential sell high but should produce in 2026.  Wills round 3 TE. Ricky Pearsall has an opportunity to break out as a WR2 but needs to stay healthy enough to do so. Signed Christian Kirk in offseason, veteran with no fantasy value but will take away targets. Kittle recovering from torn achilles. Drafted Wills the TE. Tonges should function as primary TE2, Wills will be TE3. CMC is a potential sell high candidate but know if healthy he is the number 1 overall RB in 2026.

2020.notes: Brady arrival transformed everything. Evans 1,006 yards. Godwin 840 yards. Gronk 45 catches. Stack TB.

2021.notes: Godwin torn ACL Week 14. Evans 1,035 yards. Fournette 812 rush + 454 rec. Brady elite. SB champs.

2022.notes: Brady retired/unretired. Godwin returning. Evans 1,124 yards. Fournette solid. White emerging late.

2023.notes: Mayfield surprise Pro Bowl. Evans 1,255 yards. Godwin 1,020 yards. White 64 catches. Stack TB again.

2024.notes: Godwin torn achilles again. Evans 1,000 yards again. Irving promising rookie. Mayfield solid.

2025.notes: Evans aging & injured. Godwin struggled to stay healthy. Egbuka rookie 1st round WR amazing first half of season but fell off. Jalen McMillan returned from injury and was solid. Tez Johnson rookie WR was electric on limited opportunities. Bucs struggled to run the ball. Bucky Irving had MAJOR sophomore slump that was caused by shoulder injury and stagnant offense. Baker Mayfield looked like MVP candidate but struggled second half of season. Sean Tucker saw major goalline work but nothing else. Rachaad White filled in as lead back when Irving was hurt. Cade Otton productive TE when the ball came his way.

2026.notes: Emeka Egbuka is primed for a MASSIVE breakout if Baker Mayfield has strong 2026 season. Mike Evans left team in free agency for 49ers, opening up opportunity for Jalen McMillan & Tez Johnson (young & productive WR). Chris Godwin aging & struggles to produce but could have decent low end WR2/WR3 role over next few years. Bucs signed Kenneth Gainwell in free agency who was HIGHLY productive in Pittsburgh. Strong concern for Bucky Irving owners. We will have to see how the RB split carries. Will get a better idea through the summer and camp.

2020.notes: Henry 2,027 yards legendary season. AJ Brown 1,075 yards. Tannehill efficient. Jonnu Smith TE2 value. Dynasty gold.

2021.notes: Henry torn achilles Week 8. AJ Brown 869 yards despite injuries. Julio Jones disappointment.

2022.notes: Henry 1,538 yards. AJ Brown traded to PHI. Burks disappointing. Tannehill mediocre. Full rebuild needed.

2023.notes: Henry 1,167 yards final TEN season. Hopkins solid. Levis took over. Okonkwo 53 catches. Spears promising.

2024.notes: Henry gone. Levis mediocre. Ridley disappointing again. Pollard adequate. Full rebuild.

2025.notes: Ward 1.01 franchise QB. Brutal rookie season and little to no fantasy relevance coming out of it. Offense disfunctional. Ridley complete dissapointment & got injured. Elic Ayomanor & Chimere Dike were Cam Ward's favorite targets but were not startable due to poor offense. Tony Pollard was a low end RB2 and high end RB3 some weeks if he scored, but Tyjae Spears ate into his recieving load. Tyjae struggled to stay healthy. Okonkwo was not highly relevant.

2026.notes: Titans draft Carnell Tate 1.04. Large potential for early breakout & Cam Ward to deliver at a high level. Calvin Ridley is trash but if Cam Ward can sling the rock could produce (we don't want to own Ridley). Ayomanor & Dike COULD be good if Ward produces but with Tate around now will never be WR1 on the team. Could gain relevance though. Gunnar Helm 4th round TE draft pick & projected starter could have value. Tony Pollard is aging & only has a few years left (not attractive to own). Tyjae Spears cannot stay healthy. Have to image the Titans will invest in another RB long term (Nicholas Singleton is probably not that guy either).

2020.notes: McLaurin elite despite terrible QB. Gibson 795 yards. Thomas 72 catches — sneaky dynasty TE. McKissic PPR.

2021.notes: McLaurin 1,093 yards. Samuel 77 catches. Gibson 1,000 yards. Thomas injury. McKissic 73 catches.

2022.notes: McLaurin wasted again. Robinson 797 yards as rookie. Bad QB situation. Gibson fading.

2023.notes: Howell decent. McLaurin 1,060 yards. Dotson disappointing. Robinson 797 yards. Mediocre.

2024.notes: Daniels electric. McLaurin finally great QB. Noah Brown emerged. Robinson adequate. Luke McCaffrey sleeper.

2025.notes: Daniels srtuggled with injury & so did McLaurin. Disaster season for commanders. Austin Ekeler tore achilles (cooked), & Jacory Croskey-Merritt took over lead back responsibility. Chris Rodriguez Jr functioned as RB2. Deebo Samuel Functioned as WR1 with Terry McLarin only playing 10 games. Marcus Mariota played QB most of season & offense was horrible. Croskey-Merritt did not live up to the hype but the Commanders seem to really like him. Zach Ertz was a productive TE but tore ACL at end of the season. Jaylin Lane & Luke McCaffrey will likely never have real dynasty value. Jayden Daniels WILL BOUNCE BACK & become Elite QB.

2026.notes: Williams round 3 WR could connect early with Daniels. Terry McLaurin is aging, but if he can stay healthy, he will produce over next few years. Signed TE Chig Okonkwo who could 100% produce well with Jayden Daniels but hadn't provded it with Titans yet. Jacory Croskey-Merritt is set to be 1A RB while they sign Bucs RB Rachaad White in free agency. Could function as 1B and recieving back. Very curious to see how this dynamic turns out. Brandon Aiyuk WILL SIGN with Commanders or be traded to them. Unsure if he will be good following knee surgery, but he will be in Washington next Season. Daniels top 5 SF dynasty QB. Buy him.
